introSection: Hayabusa (Japanese: はやぶさ) was a steel suspended roller coaster previously located at Tokyo Summerland in Akiruno, Tokyo, Japan. It was built by Arrow Dynamics and operated from 1992 to 2005.

sections.title: Colour scheme
sections.content: Mint green track with blue rails and grey supports.

sections.title: Incident
sections.content: An incident occurred on Hayabusa on 24 August 2005 when one of the passengers was injured by a broken cable. On 31 January 2006, the park announced its removal. It was believed that the ride never operated between these dates.

fullText:
Hayabusa (Japanese: はやぶさ) was a steel suspended roller coaster previously located at Tokyo Summerland in Akiruno, Tokyo, Japan. It was built by Arrow Dynamics and operated from 1992 to 2005.



Mint green track with blue rails and grey supports.



An incident occurred on Hayabusa on 24 August 2005 when one of the passengers was injured by a broken cable. On 31 January 2006, the park announced its removal. It was believed that the ride never operated between these dates.


 * Hayabusa on the parks website (Japanese)
